18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}

266	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
267	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
268	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
269	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
270		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
271		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
272		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
273		Now                 func() time.Time
274		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
275		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
276		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
277		Reviewer BeadReviewer
278	
279		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
280		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
281		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
282		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
283		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
284		// (escalating to land_conflict_needs_human); false means failed-but-retriable.
285		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
286	
287		// conflictAutoRecoverFn replaces the default landConflictAutoRecover. Set
288		// in tests to inject controlled recovery results without a real git repo.
289		conflictAutoRecoverFn func(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error)
290	}

293	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
294	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
295		if ch == nil {
296			return
297		}
298		select {
299		case ch <- evt:
300		default:
301		}
302	}

304	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
305	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, harness, model, profile, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64) ProgressEvent {
306		return ProgressEvent{
307			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
308			AttemptID: attemptID,
309			WorkerID:  workerID,
310			ProjectID: projectID,
311			BeadID:    beadID,

313			Model:     model,
314			Profile:   profile,
315			Phase:     phase,

317			Heartbeat: heartbeat,

320		}
321	}

323	func randomProgressID() string {
324		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
325	}
326	
327	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
328		if w.Store == nil {
329			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
330		}
331		if w.Executor == nil {
332			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
333		}
334	
335		now := w.Now
336		if now == nil {
337			now = time.Now
338		}
339		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
340		if assignee == "" {
341			assignee = "ddx"
342		}
343		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
344		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
345			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
346		}
347		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
348		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
349			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
350		}
351		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
352		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
353			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
354		}
355		harness := rcfg.Harness()
356		model := rcfg.Model()
357		profile := rcfg.Profile()
358	
359		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
360		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
361		// hookFailed tracks beads whose pre-claim hook failed on first presentation
362		// in this run. A bead in hookFailed but not attempted gets one retry: on the
363		// second hook failure it moves to attempted so nextCandidate will skip it and
364		// the loop can exit gracefully. This prevents infinite spinning when the hook
365		// always fails while still allowing transient hook failures to be retried.
366		hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{})
367	
368		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
369			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
370		}
371	
372		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
373			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
374			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
375			"harness":      harness,
376			"model":        model,
377			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
378			"assignee":     assignee,
379			"once":         runtime.Once,
380		})
381		defer func() {
382			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
383				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
384				"successes":           result.Successes,
385				"failures":            result.Failures,
386				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
387			})
388		}()
389	
390		for {
391			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
392			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
393			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
394			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
395			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
396				return result, err
397			}
398	
399			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
400			if err != nil {
401				return result, err
402			}
403			if !ok {
404				if result.Attempts == 0 {
405					result.NoReadyWork = true
406					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
407						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
408							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
409								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
410								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
411								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
412								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
413								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
414							}
415						}
416					}
417				}
418				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
419					return result, nil
420				}
421				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
422					return result, err
423				}
424				continue
425			}
426	
427			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
428			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
429			// continues (ctx is not cancelled). The bead is NOT added to
430			// attempted on the FIRST failure so a transient hook failure (e.g.
431			// diverged branch) allows a retry on the next iteration. On a
432			// SECOND failure for the same bead in the same run it is moved into
433			// attempted so nextCandidate will skip it, preventing an infinite
434			// loop when the hook always fails.
435			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
436				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
437					if runtime.Log != nil {
438						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
439					}
440					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
441						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
442						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
443					})
444					if _, alreadyFailed := hookFailed[candidate.ID]; alreadyFailed {
445						// Second failure in this run: stop retrying this bead.
446						attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
447					} else {
448						hookFailed[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
449					}
450					continue
451				}
452			}
453	
454			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
455	
456			// Routing preflight gate (FEAT-006 D3, ddx-98e6e9ef): consult the
457			// upstream typed-incompatibility surface BEFORE claiming. If the
458			// configured (harness, model) cannot serve the bead, exit the loop
459			// with a worker-level failure record — no claim, no executor
460			// invocation, no tier-attempt event burn.
461			if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
462				if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
463					detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
464						harness, model, rerr.Error())
465					if runtime.Log != nil {
466						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (skipping %s, exiting loop)\n",
467							detail, candidate.ID)
468					}
469					emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
470						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
471						"harness": harness,
472						"model":   model,
473						"reason":  rerr.Error(),
474					})
475					report := ExecuteBeadReport{
476						BeadID:  candidate.ID,
477						Status:  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
478						Detail:  detail,
479						Harness: harness,
480						Model:   model,
481					}
482					result.Attempts++
483					result.Failures++
484					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
485					result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
486					return result, nil
487				}
488			}
489	
490			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
491				continue
492			}
493	
494			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
495				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
496				"title":    candidate.Title,
497				"assignee": assignee,
498			})
499	
500			if runtime.Log != nil {
501				if candidate.Title != "" {
502					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
503				} else {
504					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
505				}
506			}
507	
508			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
509			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
510			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
511			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
512			runStart := now()
513			phaseSeq := 0
514			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
515				phaseSeq++
516				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
517					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
518					harness, model, profile,
519					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
520				))
521			}
522	
523			nextPhase("queueing", false)
524	
525			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
526			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
527			hbWG.Add(1)
528			go func(beadID string) {
529				defer hbWG.Done()
530				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
531				defer ticker.Stop()
532				for {
533					select {
534					case <-hbCtx.Done():
535						return
536					case <-ticker.C:
537						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)

1034	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
1035		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
1036		if err != nil {
1037			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
1038		}
1039		for _, candidate := range ready {
1040			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
1041				continue
1042			}
1043			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
1044				continue
1045			}
1046			return candidate, true, nil
1047		}
1048		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
1049	}

1051	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
1052	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
1053	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
1054	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
1055	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
1056		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
1057			return
1058		}
1059		provider := report.Provider
1060		if provider == "" {
1061			provider = report.Harness
1062		}
1063		if provider == "" {
1064			return
1065		}
1066		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{

1255	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
1256	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
1257	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
1258	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
1259	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
1260	//     embed the class in their message).
1261	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
1262	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
1263	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
1264	//     matches.
1265	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
1266		if reviewRes != nil {
1267			switch reviewRes.Error {
1268			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1269				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
1270				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
1271				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
1272				return reviewRes.Error
1273			}
1274		}
1275		msg := ""
1276		if reviewErr != nil {
1277			msg = reviewErr.Error()
1278		}
1279		switch {
1280		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
1281			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
1282		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
1283			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1284		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
1285			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1286		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
1287			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1288		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):

1290		}

1372	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runConflictRecovery(

1401

1414				report.ResultRev = resolvedTip
1415				report.Detail = "auto-recovered: focused conflict-resolve agent landed preserved iteration"

46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3

51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}

105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}

51	// TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim covers ddx-98e6e9ef:
52	// when RoutePreflight returns the upstream typed-incompatibility error
53	// (agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible), the loop must NOT claim the bead,
54	// must NOT invoke the executor, must record a worker-level execution_failed
55	// result naming the rejected (harness, model) pair, and must exit.
56	func TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim(t *testing.T) {
57		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
58		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
59	
60		var execCount int32
61		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
62			Store: store,
63			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
64				atomic.AddInt32(&execCount, 1)
65				return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess}, nil
66			}),
67		}
68	
69		// Fabricate a typed-incompatibility error via the upstream test seam:
70		// the preflight callback returns the same error type that upstream
71		// ResolveRoute returns when a harness allow-list rejects the model.
72		rejected := agentlib.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible{
73			Harness:         "claude",
74			Model:           "gpt-5",
75			SupportedModels: []string{"claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
76		}
77		var preflightCalls int32
78		preflight := func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
79			atomic.AddInt32(&preflightCalls, 1)
80			return rejected
81		}
82	
83		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
84			Assignee: "worker",
85			Harness:  "claude",
86			Model:    "gpt-5",
87		}
88		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
89	
90		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
91			Once:           true,
92			RoutePreflight: preflight,
93		})
94		require.NoError(t, err)
95		require.NotNil(t, result)
96	
97		// AC#1: preflight ran before Claim.
98		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightCalls), "preflight must be invoked")
99	
100		// AC#2: Claim was NEVER called on the store.
101		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls),
102			"beadStore.Claim must not be called when preflight rejects")
103	
104		// Executor must not have been invoked either.
105		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCount),
106			"executor must not run when preflight rejects")
107	
108		// AC#3: worker-level execution_failed record names the rejected pair.
109		require.Len(t, result.Results, 1, "exactly one worker-level failure record")
110		report := result.Results[0]
111		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, report.Status)
112		assert.Equal(t, candidate.ID, report.BeadID)
113		assert.Equal(t, "claude", report.Harness)
114		assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5", report.Model)
115		assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "claude", "detail must name rejected harness")
116		assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "gpt-5", "detail must name rejected model")
117		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, result.LastFailureStatus)
118		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
119	
120		// AC#4: bead has no kind:tier-attempt event from this attempt.
121		events, err := store.Events(candidate.ID)
122		require.NoError(t, err)
123		for _, ev := range events {
124			assert.NotEqual(t, "tier-attempt", ev.Kind,
125				"no tier-attempt event must be recorded when preflight rejects pre-claim")
126		}
127	
128		// Bead must remain open with no owner — confirms no Claim side-effects.
129		got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
130		require.NoError(t, err)
131		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
132		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
133	}

# Complexity Evaluator — Triage Gate

You are evaluating a bead (work item) to decide whether it is safe to dispatch
to an agent worker as a single unit of work, or whether it must be decomposed
into smaller child beads first.

## Input

You will receive:
- `title`: the bead title
- `description`: the bead body / motivation
- `acceptance`: the acceptance-criteria list
- `prior_attempts`: list of prior attempt summaries (status, rationale)
- `labels`: current labels
- `depth`: current decomposition depth (0 = root)

## Classification

Respond with a JSON object:

```json
{
  "classification": "<atomic|decomposable|ambiguous>",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "reasoning": "one or two sentences"
}
```

### atomic

Choose **atomic** when:
- The acceptance criteria map to a **single coherent change** (one PR, one
  logical unit of work).
- A single agent can complete the bead in one session without needing to make
  parallel decisions about unrelated subsystems.
- Criteria such as "add X", "fix Y", "update Z" that all touch the same
  concern.

### decomposable

Choose **decomposable** when:
- The acceptance criteria list **independent deliverables** that could each be
  a standalone PR (e.g. "endpoint A", "endpoint B", "dashboard C").
- The bead title contains epic-scope language: "all", "complete suite",
  "initial tranche", "batch", "matrix", "full system", "multiple".
- Prior attempts returned `no_changes` with rationale containing "epic",
  "split", "breakdown", "scope", or "monolithic" — this is a strong signal.
- The description enumerates phases, stages, or distinct workstreams.
- Attempt count ≥ 3 on a bead with no merged commits — suggests the scope is
  unmanageable as a single unit.

### ambiguous

Choose **ambiguous** only when you genuinely cannot determine atomicity from
the available information and need human clarification. Prefer a confident
classification over marking ambiguous when evidence favours one side.

## Examples

**atomic** — "Fix nil pointer dereference in Execute when model string is empty"
Reasoning: single bug fix in one function.

**decomposable** — "Harness × Model Matrix Benchmark — initial tranche"
Reasoning: 'matrix' and 'initial tranche' signal multiple distinct benchmark
runs that should be separate beads; prior attempt returned no_changes citing
"monolithic".

**atomic** — "Add --dry-run flag to ddx work command"
Reasoning: single flag addition in one file; one PR.

**decomposable** — "Implement all CRUD operations for the user resource"
Reasoning: four independent endpoints (create, read, update, delete), each a
separate PR; 'all' + multiple discrete AC items.

## Output format

Return **only** the JSON object. No prose before or after.

# Bead Splitter — Triage Gate

You are decomposing a coarse bead (epic) into focused child beads that can
each be dispatched to an agent worker as a single unit of work.

## Input

You will receive the parent bead:
- `title`: the bead title
- `description`: the bead body / motivation
- `acceptance`: the acceptance-criteria list
- `labels`: current labels
- `spec_id`: inherited spec reference (if any)

## Task

Produce N child bead specs (JSON array) that together:
1. **Cover all acceptance-criteria** from the parent — each AC item must appear
   in at least one child's `acceptance` field.
2. Are **independently deliverable** — a single agent should be able to close
   each child bead in one session.
3. Are **mutually exclusive in scope** — no child duplicates another child's
   work.
4. Inherit the parent `spec_id` unless a child has a more specific spec.

## Output format

Return a JSON object with two fields:

```json
{
  "rationale": "one sentence explaining the split strategy",
  "children": [
    {
      "title": "...",
      "description": "...",
      "acceptance": "...",
      "labels": [],
      "spec_id": "",
      "in_scope_files": []
    }
  ]
}
```

- `title`: concise, action-oriented (verb-noun).
- `description`: one paragraph, motivation only.
- `acceptance`: the subset of parent AC that belongs to this child, verbatim or
  slightly refined.
- `labels`: inherit parent labels that apply; add child-specific ones if needed.
- `spec_id`: inherit parent spec_id unless a more specific spec applies.
- `in_scope_files`: file paths or glob patterns the agent should focus on.
  Leave empty when scope is not file-bounded.

## Constraints

- Minimum 2 children; maximum 7 children per split.
- Each child must have a non-empty `title` and `acceptance`.
- Do not rewrite or summarise the parent AC — preserve exact wording for
  traceability.
- Children are filed as siblings; the triage gate handles parent-linking and
  depth tracking automatically.

## Output

Return **only** the JSON object. No prose before or after.

# Triage Gate — Pre-Claim Complexity Evaluator

The triage gate is a pre-Claim phase in `ddx work` that evaluates each candidate
bead before an agent worker claims it. Its purpose is to prevent coarse epics
(beads with multiple independent deliverables) from being dispatched as
monolithic work items, which wastes attempt slots and corrupts claim semantics.

## When the gate triggers

The gate runs for every ready bead, between the routing preflight check and
`Store.Claim`. It evaluates the bead's title, description, acceptance criteria,
and prior attempt history to classify it as:

- **atomic** — a single coherent unit of work; proceeds to Claim normally.
- **decomposable** — multiple independent deliverables; the gate files child
  beads and blocks the parent.
- **ambiguous** — needs human clarification; the gate sets
  `execution-eligible=false` and adds `label=triage:needs-human`.

## Bypassing the gate per-bead

Add the label `triage:skip` to a bead to bypass the complexity gate entirely:

```
ddx bead update <id> --label triage:skip
```

The bead will proceed directly to Claim on the next dispatch cycle. Use this
for beads you have manually reviewed and confirmed are atomic, or for beads
that the gate misclassifies.

## Decomposed parent state

When the gate decomposes a bead:

1. Child beads are filed with `parent: <id>` linking back to the epic.
2. The parent's status is set to `blocked`.
3. The parent receives a `kind:triage-decomposed` event whose JSON body lists
   the `child_ids` and the splitter's `rationale`.
4. Dependency edges are added: the parent depends on all children, ensuring it
   cannot be dispatched again until children close (should it be re-opened).

Children re-enter the triage gate on their next dispatch cycle. If a child is
itself decomposable, the gate will split it further — up to the depth cap.

## Recursion depth cap

Default depth cap: **3** levels. Configurable in `.ddx/config.yaml`:

```yaml
agent:
  triage:
    max_decomposition_depth: 3
```

When a bead at the depth cap is evaluated, the gate:

1. Appends a `kind:triage-overflow` event.
2. Sets `status=blocked` with `label=needs-human-decomposition`.
3. Does **not** invoke the classifier or splitter.
4. Does **not** dispatch the bead.

The operator must manually split the bead or add `triage:skip` to bypass.

## AC-coverage metric

The `bead-split` prompt is evaluated against a held-out corpus using a
string-overlap metric: the fraction of unique AC tokens (alphanumeric tokens
≥ 3 chars) from the parent's acceptance criteria that appear in the combined
acceptance criteria of the child specs. A rate ≥ 90% is required.

## Regenerating the historical corpus

The frozen eval slice lives in `library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl`. To
regenerate from live bead history:

```bash
go run scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go \
  -beads   .ddx/beads.jsonl \
  -events  .ddx/events.jsonl \
  -out     library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl \
  -seed    42
```

The harvest script produces a deterministic, seeded 80/20 train/eval split.
Running it with the same inputs and seed always produces the same corpus file.

## Disabling the gate

The gate is disabled when `ExecuteBeadWorker.ComplexityGate == nil`. This is
intentionally non-silent: the worker emits a one-time warning per boot:

```
warning: triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts
```

The gate should only be disabled in test fixtures or during development. In
production, always wire a `ComplexityGate` via `NewComplexityGate`.

## Tracker events

| Event kind              | When                                       | Body                          |
|-------------------------|--------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| `triage-decomposed`     | Parent split into children                 | `{child_ids, rationale}`      |
| `triage-overflow`       | Bead at depth cap, blocked                 | `{depth, max}`                |
| `triage-ambiguous`      | Gate returned ambiguous classification     | `{confidence, reasoning}`     |
| `triage.gate_disabled`  | Loop boot with nil gate (loop event log)   | `{bead_id}`                   |
| `triage.skipped`        | Gate returned shouldClaim=false            | `{bead_id}`                   |
| `triage.error`          | Gate returned a non-nil error              | `{bead_id, reason}`           |

7. **Operator workaround retired:** the README's "if `ddx work` exits early, restart it" guidance (or equivalent) is removed; that workaround was masking this bug.","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:38.552407002Z","description":"## Symptom

`ddx work` (no `--once` flag, no `--poll-interval`, default mode) exits after processing exactly one bead instead of draining the queue. Reproduced four times today on `/home/erik/Projects/agent` with `--harness lmstudio` pinned (which sidesteps the unrelated `ddx-c7081f89` server-worker tier-walk issue):

| Run | Outcome on first bead | Worker terminal state | Queue still non-empty? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | `already_satisfied` (`agent-4aa3584e` adjudicated satisfied) | `already_satisfied` / `exited` | yes — 2 ready beads remained |
| 3 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |
| 4 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |
| 5 | `no_changes` from agent (`agent-6c7a4c11` declined as epic; bead left **claimed**) | `exited`, attempts=1 | yes — 5 ready beads remained |

In all four runs the loop should have continued to the next ready bead. Per `agent/.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, only `success`/`already_satisfied` close the bead and `no_changes`/`land_conflict`/`*_failed` unclaim and continue. None of these outcomes is documented or expected to be loop-terminal.

## Root cause #1 (verified)

`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:382`:

```go
attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}

// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
    if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
        // ... log + emit ...
        continue
    }
}
```

The bead is added to `attempted` **before** the pre-claim hook runs. On hook failure (e.g. diverged branch), the loop `continue`s but the bead stays in `attempted`. Next iteration, `nextCandidate()` (line 924) skips the still-ready bead because it's in `attempted`, returns `ok=false`, and the loop exits at line 374 because `PollInterval <= 0`. Single-shot exit with `attempts=0`.

**Reproduces:** runs 3 and 4 above.

## Root cause #2 (suspected — needs targeted instrumentation)

Runs 2 and 5 reached `Execute()` and got a real report back. Worker log shows the entry line `▶ <bead>: <title>` but no closing `✓ <bead> → <result>` line (which is written at line 909). That means the loop exited somewhere between line 488 (`Executor.Execute`) and line 909 (the final summary log).

Between those bounds, **at least 16 distinct `return result, err` points** trigger if any `Store.*` operation returns an error: `Unclaim` (711, 700), `IncrNoChangesCount` (717), `adjudicateNoChanges` (721), `CloseWithEvidence` (520, 615, 694, 739), `Reopen` (635, 669), `SetExecutionCooldown` (749, 791, 803, 835, 844), late `AppendEvent` (862). Any single transient store error during outcome handling kills the worker.

For run 5 specifically, `agent-6c7a4c11` is now `Status: in_progress` with `Owner: ddx, Machine: sindri`. The bead was **never unclaimed** despite the agent returning `no_changes` — meaning the loop exited on or before line 711 (`w.Store.Unclaim`) failing. Investigation should look at what error `Unclaim` would return when called against a bead the same worker claimed seconds earlier; possible candidates: tracker-write race with concurrent worker, bead.jsonl re-write during a fetch by the pre-claim hook on a later iteration that already happened (no — pre-claim is pre-claim, not post-execute), or a malformed bead row that fails store-side validation.

Independent of the specific store error: the structural pattern of "any Store.* error → exit the entire loop" is the wrong default. A store error on outcome handling should at most park the bead and continue to the next, never terminate the worker.

## Why this is loadbearing

The loop is the queue-drain primitive. Every observed failure-to-drain in the last 24 hours of operator complaints traces back to this — beads that should have been the worker's second/third/fourth attempt never got picked up because the worker exited after one attempt, and the operator manually restarts the drain (sometimes after a long delay). The visible queue depth is misleading: half the "ready" beads are sitting behind a worker that quietly died.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — fixes for both root causes
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go` — regression tests for each path

## Out of scope

- Anything in the `agent` SDK (`/home/erik/Projects/agent`). The loop and all its store interactions are owned by ddx.
- The pre-claim hook's divergence-detection logic itself — that's correct behavior; the bug is how the loop reacts to the hook's signal.
- Adaptive min-tier / tier-walk machinery (`ddx-c7081f89`, `ddx-aec9d68c`) — independent.

## Anti-goals

- Do **not** swallow store errors silently. They must be surfaced (event + log) but should not kill the worker on transient/per-bead failures.
- Do **not** convert the loop's Once/PollInterval semantics — they're correct.

{"acceptance":"1. `complexityGate` phase wired into `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` pre-Claim. Unit test asserts: a known-decomposable bead from the historical corpus does NOT reach `Claim`; a known-atomic bead does.

2. Both prompts (`library/prompts/triage/complexity-eval.md`, `library/prompts/triage/bead-split.md`) exist and pass `go test ./cli/internal/agent -run TriagePrompts`, which exercises them against the held-out eval set via the embedded agent harness.

3. `complexity-eval` classifier achieves >= 80% accuracy on the held-out eval slice. The corpus and eval slice are reproducibly built from `.ddx/beads.jsonl` via `scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go`; running the script regenerates the same eval set deterministically (sorted, seeded split).

4. `bead-split` produces child beads whose collective AC covers >= 90% of the parent's AC tokens (string-overlap metric documented in `docs/triage/decomposition.md`), measured on the held-out eval slice.

5. Recursion depth cap = 3 by default, configurable via `agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth`. At cap, parent is marked `status=blocked` with `label=needs-human-decomposition` and a `kind:triage-overflow` event is filed. Test asserts: a synthetic always-decomposable bead reaches the cap and the parent is blocked, never dispatched.

6. End-to-end: run `ddx work --once` against a fixture queue containing the historical body of `agent-6c7a4c11` (or an equivalent epic). Assert: (a) gate classifies decomposable, (b) children are filed with parent link, (c) parent is not dispatched, (d) the first atomic child IS dispatched.

7. Operator docs at `docs/triage/decomposition.md` cover: when the gate triggers, how to opt out per-bead (`ddx bead update <id> --label triage:skip`), how to configure depth cap, and how to regenerate the historical corpus.

8. Tracker: parent epic has `kind:triage-decomposed` event listing child IDs; children carry `parent: <id>` and inherit `spec-id` from parent.","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T21:19:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"932496","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:00:05.617369837Z","description":"## Goal

Today, when execute-loop dispatches a coarse bead (an unsplit epic) to an agent, the agent often returns `no_changes` with reasoning like "this is an epic, needs child-bead breakdown, not monolithic execution" — wasting a worker attempt, leaving the bead in a half-owned claimed state, and forcing manual operator intervention.

Concrete recent example: `agent-6c7a4c11` (Harness × Model Matrix Benchmark — initial tranche) on this user's `agent` repo was dispatched as a single bead, the agent declined to execute monolithically, the worker exited without unclaiming. The bead is now stuck claimed by a dead worker with no change applied.

The fix is to run a triage step **before** Claim: evaluate whether the bead is atomic (one PR's worth of work, single AC focus) or decomposable (multiple distinct deliverables, AC items map to separable PRs). If decomposable, run a splitter that produces child beads (parent-linked, AC-distributed), and skip dispatch of the parent. Children themselves re-enter triage — recursive decomposition with a depth cap.

## Concrete change

Insert a `complexityGate` phase in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` between bead-pick and Claim:

```
next-candidate → ResolveRoute → preflight gate (ddx-98e6e9ef)
  → complexityGate (NEW)
  → if atomic:        Claim → invoke
  → if decomposable:  splitter → file children with parent link → mark parent blocked-by-children → re-pick
  → if ambiguous:     surface to human triage (event + label), don't claim
```

Two prompts, both shipped with the default DDx plugin under `library/prompts/triage/`:

1. **`complexity-eval.md`** — given bead body + AC + deps + history (prior attempts, reviewer BLOCKs), classify `{atomic, decomposable, ambiguous}` with reasoning + confidence.
2. **`bead-split.md`** — given a decomposable bead, emit N child beads (JSON) with: title, AC, parent link, spec-id inheritance, in-scope files subset, labels. Children must collectively cover the parent's AC.

Both prompts use the embedded `agent` harness with profile=default (cheap tier, local-first) — the gate's cost/latency must be << a wasted dispatch.

## Historical evidence engineering

Both prompts must be engineered against historical evidence, not vibes. Harvest a corpus from `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (this project + the user's `agent` project, which has 5 months of bead history including known epic-rejection cases):

- Beads closed `no_changes` whose status text mentions "epic", "split", "breakdown", "scope", or "monolithic" → positive examples for **decomposable**.
- Beads with `attempt_count >= 3` or repeated `kind:reopen` events from reviewer BLOCKs citing scope → positive examples for **decomposable**.
- Beads that were eventually split manually (parent epic + N child tasks filed in a single tracker batch) → use parent body as splitter input, the actual children as ground-truth output.
- Beads closed cleanly on first attempt with single-file or small-diff commits → positive examples for **atomic**.

Hold out 20% as eval. Report `complexity-eval` accuracy/F1 on classification; report `bead-split` AC-coverage rate (children's AC tokens together cover parent's AC) and child-count distribution vs. ground-truth.

The harvest script (`scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go` or equivalent) is committed and reproducible.

## Recursion + depth cap

Children re-enter the gate. Default depth cap = 3, configurable via `agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth` in `.ddx/config.yaml`. At cap: surface to human (`kind:triage-overflow` event, `status=blocked`, `label=needs-human-decomposition`). Never silently dispatch a parent that hit cap.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — new `complexityGate` phase pre-Claim
- `cli/internal/agent/triage.go` (new) — gate logic, prompt invocation, child filing
- `library/prompts/triage/complexity-eval.md` (new)
- `library/prompts/triage/bead-split.md` (new)
- `cli/internal/agent/triage_test.go` (new) — unit tests against held-out historical corpus
- `library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl` (new) — frozen eval slice (training slice can be regenerated)
- `scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go` (new) — corpus regeneration
- `docs/triage/decomposition.md` (new) — operator docs

## Out of scope (file follow-ups if/when needed)

- Backfill triage on already-claimed in-flight beads (e.g. unsticking agent-6c7a4c11 retroactively) — operational bead, not part of this feature.
- A web UI for visualizing the triage decision per bead — consumer bead.
- Per-team customization of split rules — premature; ship the default and iterate.

## Anti-goals

- Do **not** rewrite an existing bead in place. Splitting always files children; the parent stays as an epic with `status=blocked-by-children`.
- Do **not** make the gate silently optional. If disabled via flag or config, emit a one-time warning per worker boot.
- Do **not** use a model larger than the cheap tier for the gate prompts.
- Do **not** duplicate routing logic in the gate; it should be a thin caller of the agent SDK with the gate's two prompts.

## Why P1

The failure mode it prevents — claimed-but-undeliverable beads — corrupts the queue's claim semantics, wastes operator time, and silently inflates `attempt_count` on epics that will never close. Without this gate, the rest of the routing-cleanup work (`ddx-fdd3ea36` umbrella) ships into a queue with structurally bad inputs.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-29T21:19:09.550246681Z","id":"ddx-5bf4ee7e","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:dispatch","area:bead-triage","kind:feat","phase:design"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"status":"in_progress","title":"pre-claim triage: complexity evaluator + auto-splitter for recursive epic decomposition before agent dispatch","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:19:09.550246681Z"}

334			"../../../library/prompts/triage/bead-split.md",
335		} {
336			data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
337			require.NoError(t, err, "prompt file must exist: %s", path)
338			assert.NotEmpty(t, data, "prompt file must be non-empty: %s", path)
339		}
340	}
341	
342	// TestTriagePromptsAccuracy loads the held-out eval slice from
343	// eval-corpus.jsonl and runs RuleBasedClassifier (which encodes the
344	// complexity-eval prompt criteria) against each entry. Asserts >= 80%
345	// accuracy, satisfying AC-3.
346	func TestTriagePromptsAccuracy(t *testing.T) {
347		corpus, err := loadEvalCorpus("../../../library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl")
348		require.NoError(t, err, "eval-corpus.jsonl must load without error")
349		require.NotEmpty(t, corpus, "eval-corpus.jsonl must contain at least one entry")
350	
351		evalSlice := evalHoldout(corpus)
352		require.NotEmpty(t, evalSlice, "eval holdout must contain at least one entry")
353	
354		correct := 0
355		for _, item := range evalSlice {
356			got, _, _, err := RuleBasedClassifier(context.Background(), item.Bead)
357			require.NoError(t, err)
358			if got == item.Label {
359				correct++
360			}
361		}
362	
363		accuracy := float64(correct) / float64(len(evalSlice))
364		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, accuracy, 0.80,
365			"complexity-eval accuracy must be >= 80%% on eval slice (got %.0f%%); eval items: %v",
366			accuracy*100, evalSliceLabels(evalSlice))
367	}
368	
369	// TestTriagePromptsACCoverage verifies that the ground-truth children in the
370	// eval corpus collectively cover >= 90% of their parent's AC tokens, satisfying
371	// AC-4 (bead-split AC-coverage metric).
372	func TestTriagePromptsACCoverage(t *testing.T) {
373		corpus, err := loadEvalCorpus("../../../library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl")
374		require.NoError(t, err)
375	
376		evalSlice := evalHoldout(corpus)
377		var coverages []float64
378		for _, item := range evalSlice {
379			if item.Label != TriageClassificationDecomposable || len(item.GroundTruthChildren) == 0 {
380				continue
381			}
382			rate := ACCoverageRate(item.Bead.Acceptance, item.GroundTruthChildren)
383			coverages = append(coverages, rate)
384		}
385		if len(coverages) == 0 {
386			t.Skip("no decomposable examples with ground-truth children in eval slice")
387		}
388	
389		total := 0.0
390		for _, r := range coverages {
391			total += r
392		}
393		avg := total / float64(len(coverages))
394		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, avg, 0.90,
395			"average AC coverage must be >= 90%% on eval slice (got %.0f%%)", avg*100)
396	}
397	
398	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
399	// Helpers.
400	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
401	
402	// corpusEntry is one row of the eval-corpus.jsonl file.
403	type corpusEntry struct {
404		ID                  string          `json:"id"`
405		Label               string          `json:"label"`
406		Bead                bead.Bead       `json:"bead"`
407		GroundTruthChildren []ChildBeadSpec `json:"ground_truth_children"`
408	}
409	
410	// loadEvalCorpus reads and parses the JSONL eval corpus file.
411	func loadEvalCorpus(path string) ([]corpusEntry, error) {
412		f, err := os.Open(path)
413		if err != nil {
414			return nil, err
415		}
416		defer f.Close()
417	
418		var entries []corpusEntry
419		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
420		for sc.Scan() {
421			line := sc.Bytes()
422			if len(line) == 0 {
423				continue
424			}
425			var e corpusEntry
426			if err := json.Unmarshal(line, &e); err != nil {
427				return nil, err
428			}
429			entries = append(entries, e)
430		}
431		return entries, sc.Err()
432	}
433	
434	// evalHoldout returns the held-out eval slice from a corpus: entries are
435	// sorted by ID and every 5th entry (1-indexed) is selected. This produces a
436	// deterministic, reproducible 20% holdout.
437	func evalHoldout(corpus []corpusEntry) []corpusEntry {
438		sorted := make([]corpusEntry, len(corpus))
439		copy(sorted, corpus)
440		sort.Slice(sorted, func(i, j int) bool { return sorted[i].ID < sorted[j].ID })
441	
442		var eval []corpusEntry
443		for i, e := range sorted {
444			if (i+1)%5 == 0 {
445				eval = append(eval, e)
446			}
447		}
448		return eval
449	}
450	
451	// evalSliceLabels returns a list of "id:label" strings for diagnostics.
452	func evalSliceLabels(slice []corpusEntry) []string {
453		out := make([]string, len(slice))
454		for i, e := range slice {
455			out[i] = e.ID + ":" + e.Label
456		}
457		return out
458	}
459	
460	// countOccurrences returns the number of non-overlapping occurrences of sub
461	// in s.
462	func countOccurrences(s, sub string) int {
463		n := 0
464		for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(sub); {
465			if s[i:i+len(sub)] == sub {
466				n++
467				i += len(sub)
468			} else {
469				i++
470			}
471		}
472		return n
473	}

487		// Empty parent AC → 100% coverage by convention.

489	
490		// Zero overlap.
491		noOverlap := []ChildBeadSpec{{Acceptance: "xyz"}}
492		assert.Less(t, ACCoverageRate("aaa bbb ccc ddd", noOverlap), 0.10)
493	}

506			{"int64", map[string]any{TriageDepthKey: int64(1)}, 1},
507		}
508		for _, tc := range cases {

510				b := bead.Bead{Extra: tc.extra}
511				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, triageDepth(b))
512			})
513		}
514	}
